chokepoint.name: Cyclone Hamoon · Arabian Sea
chokepoint.summary: Category 3 cyclone, max sustained winds 105 kt, moving NNE at 14 kt. Cone of uncertainty covers central Arabian Sea. Seas 8–10 m in danger zone. Forecast track unstable — landfall probability elevated along Gujarat / South Konkan coast.

advisory.title: Cyclone Hamoon · Arabian Sea
advisory.summary: Category 3 tropical cyclone on unstable northerly track. Direct route passes within 180 nm of forecast centre. IMD advisory: vessels should not enter sea areas within 250 nm of cyclone centre. Cargo manifest includes humanitarian aid.

sources.title: Cyclone forces cargo reroutes in Arabian Sea
IMO circular on tropical cyclone evasion

portCongestion.note: CICT has priority humanitarian berth available; port authority pre-notified for aid cargo. Minimal wait regardless of diversion route chosen.

decision.headline: Divert via southern bypass · clear of cyclone track
decision.rationale: Direct route enters IMD's 250 nm exclusion zone during forecast peak intensity. Cyclone track is unstable — any northward wobble would place the vessel inside the danger radius with no sea room. Southern bypass adds 19 h and $34K in fuel to deliver humanitarian cargo safely to Colombo. Coastal hug is faster than the southern bypass but remains tangentially exposed to swells and outer bands — acceptable only if southern berths are congested. Direct transit: not authorised under IMD advisory.
